title: Electric Vehicles Accelerate Forward
subtitle: 电动汽车革命正在重新定义交通出行

paragraphs.en: Electric vehicles have transitioned from niche curiosity to mainstream transportation option. Major automakers have committed billions to electrification, with some announcing plans to phase out internal combustion engines entirely within the next decade. This transformation represents the most significant shift in automotive history since the Model T.
paragraphs.zh: 电动汽车已从小众新奇事物转变为主流交通选择。各大汽车制造商已承诺投入数十亿美元用于电气化，一些公司宣布计划在未来十年内完全淘汰内燃机。这一转型代表着自T型车以来汽车史上最重大的变革。

paragraphs.en: Battery technology improvements have addressed earlier concerns about limited driving range. Modern electric vehicles can travel over 400 kilometers on a single charge, with charging infrastructure expanding rapidly along highways and in urban centers. Fast-charging stations can replenish batteries to eighty percent capacity in under thirty minutes.
paragraphs.zh: 电池技术的进步已经解决了早期对续航里程有限的担忧。现代电动汽车单次充电可行驶超过400公里，充电基础设施正在高速公路沿线和城市中心快速扩展。快速充电站可以在三十分钟内将电池充至百分之八十的容量。

paragraphs.en: Environmental benefits drive much of the enthusiasm for electric vehicles. Transportation accounts for a significant portion of global carbon emissions, and electrification offers a path to cleaner air in cities. However, the environmental impact depends heavily on how electricity is generated in each region.
paragraphs.zh: 环境效益是推动电动汽车热情的主要动力。交通运输占全球碳排放的很大一部分，电气化为城市提供了一条通向更清洁空气的道路。然而，环境影响在很大程度上取决于每个地区的电力生产方式。

paragraphs.en: The transition creates both opportunities and challenges for economies worldwide. New jobs emerge in battery manufacturing and software development, while traditional automotive workers face displacement. Countries rich in lithium and cobalt—essential battery materials—gain geopolitical significance.
paragraphs.zh: 这一转型为世界各地的经济创造了机遇和挑战。电池制造和软件开发领域出现了新的工作岗位，而传统汽车工人则面临失业。富含锂和钴——关键电池材料——的国家获得了地缘政治上的重要性。

paragraphs.en: Consumer attitudes continue evolving as electric vehicles become more affordable and practical. Government incentives, including tax credits and subsidies, accelerate adoption rates. Within a generation, the gasoline-powered car may become as obsolete as the horse-drawn carriage, a relic of a carbon-intensive past.
paragraphs.zh: 随着电动汽车变得更加经济实用，消费者态度持续转变。政府激励措施，包括税收抵免和补贴，加速了采用率。在一代人的时间内，汽油动力汽车可能会变得像马车一样过时，成为碳密集型过去的遗物。
